title.en: That's awful! I'm so sorry to hear that! 너무 안타깝다! 정말 안됐다!
title.ko: 너무 안타깝다! 정말 안됐다! 진짜 끔찍하네!

description.en: When you truly empathize and comfort someone regarding bad news! Practice offering warm comfort with 너무 안타깝다 and 정말 안됐다.
description.ko: 상대방에게 일어난 좋지 않은 일에 진심으로 공감하고 위로할 때! 너무 안타깝다와 정말 안됐다를 사용해 따뜻한 위로를 전해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Empathy. Sympathy. Comfort. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 저 오늘 정말 속상한 일이 있었어요.
content.translation: John. something really upsetting happened to me today.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제가 제일 아끼는 가방을 지하철에 두고 내렸거든요.
content.translation: I left my favorite bag on the subway.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh no! That's awful! 너무 안타깝다. 그거 정말 아끼는 가방이었잖아요.
content.translation: Oh no! That's awful! It's so unfortunate. That was your favorite bag.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 찾을 수 있을까요?
content.translation: I hope you can find it.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 분실물 센터에 전화해 봤는데 아직 소식이 없네요.
content.translation: I called the lost and found center. but there's no news yet.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 안됐다. 속상해서 어떡해요. 제가 같이 한 번 더 알아봐 줄게요.
content.translation: That's too bad. I'm so sorry. I'll help you look into it one more time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 안타깝다. This is used when you feel sad for someone else's situation. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 어떤 상황이 생각대로 되지 않거나 누군가에게 손해나 손실이 생겼을 때 내 마음도 아프다는 걸 보여주는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It's an expression to show that your heart hurts too when a situation doesn't go as planned or when someone suffers a loss.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 안타깝다.
content.translation: It's so unfortunate.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 정말 안됐다 라는 말은 상대방이 겪은 불행이나 안 좋은 일에 대해 가여워하는 마음을 담아 쓰는 리액션이에요.
content.translation: And the phrase 'Jeong-mal an-dwaet-da' is a reaction used to show pity for the misfortune or bad thing someone experienced.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 안됐다.
content.translation: I'm so sorry to hear that.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about when something is really terrible or shocking.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 진짜 끔찍하네 라고 말해 보세요. 뉴스에서 아주 나쁜 소식을 들었거나 정말 말도 안 되는 안 좋은 상황을 봤을 때 써요.
content.translation: In that case. try saying 'Jin-jja kkeum-jjik-ha-ne'. It's used when you hear very bad news on the news or see a truly nonsensical bad situation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I'm telling you about a car accident I saw on the news this morning.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 오늘 아침 뉴스 봤어요? 큰 사고가 났다고 하더라고요.
content.translation: John. did you see the news this morning? I heard there was a big accident.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I saw it. The whole building was damaged and many people lost their homes.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와... 진짜 끔찍하네. 어떻게 이런 일이 일어날 수 있죠?
content.translation: Wow... that's really terrible. How could something like this happen?

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's a tragedy. Many people are trying to help. but it's still heart-breaking.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 너무 안타깝다. 다들 빨리 일상으로 돌아갈 수 있으면 좋겠어요.
content.translation: It's really so unfortunate. I hope everyone can return to their daily lives soon.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I feel the same way.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Showing empathy can be a great comfort to those who are hurting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 누군가 슬픈 일을 겪었을 때 오늘 배운 표현들로 진심 어린 위로를 건네 보세요.
content.translation: Right. When someone goes through a sad time. offer sincere comfort with the expressions we learned today.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 말 한마디가 큰 힘이 될 수 있어요.
content.translation: A single word can be a great strength.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "awful" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너무 안타깝다!
content.translation: That's so unfortunate!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 안됐다!
content.translation: That's too bad!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜 끔찍하네!
content.translation: That's really awful!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 유감이다!
content.translation: I'm sorry to hear that!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 마음이 아프다!
content.translation: It breaks my heart!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 주변에 힘든 친구가 있다면 따뜻한 한마디를 건네는 하루 되시길 바랄게요.
content.translation: I hope you all have a day where you offer a warm word to any friends around you who are having a hard time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.meaning: To be unfortunate. To be pitiful
vocabulary.example: 도움이 되지 못해 정말 안타까워요. (I feel so unfortunate because I couldn't be of help.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be terrible. To be awful
vocabulary.example: 사고 현장이 정말 끔찍했어요. (The accident scene was truly terrible.)
그런 소식을 듣게 되어 유감입니다. (I'm sorry to hear such news.)
친구의 따뜻한 위로가 힘이 되었어요. (My friend's warm comfort gave me strength.)
불행한 사고가 일어나지 않길 바래요. (I hope no unfortunate accidents happen.)
그것은 정말 슬픈 비극이었습니다. (It was a truly sad tragedy.)
